chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH CIRCUIT COURT MIRPURKHAS Constitution Petition No.D-657 of 2024 (Muhammad Murad Vs. Akbar Ali & others) DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE Before; Adnan-ul-Karim Memon, J Amjad Ali Bohio, J Date of hearing & Order 26.08.2024 Petitioner is present in person. Mr. Nabi Bux Narejo, Advocate a/w respondent No.1. Mr. Muhammad Sharif Solangi Assistant A.G., Sindh, a/w Ghulam Hussain Assistant Commissioner Mirpurkhas and Mr. Rafique Ahmed Sehar, Municipal Commissioner Mirpurkhas. = O R D E R Adnan-ul-Karim Memon, J. The petitioner Muhammad Murad, through the instant petition under Article 199 of the Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973, has assailed the Judgment dated 17-10-2023 passed by learned Presiding Officer, Anti Encroachment Tribunal Mirpurkhas in Suit No.02 of 2023, (Re- Muhammad Murad Vs. Akbar Ali and others), whereby his suit was dismissed. 2. This court vide order dated 19.08.2024 directed the Additional Registrar of this court to inspect the site and submit his report about the encroachment on the subject site. He submitted his report that the respondent had made steps of the slops of his house. He further submitted that 80% encroachment had been removed previously and during the past two months, the respondents have encroached upon the area. The respondent present in court has submitted that he has only made slops on the gate, as such there is no major encroachment over the street. Let the encroachment, if any, as pointed out be removed forthwith; however, if there is a need for certain steps to enter into the house, the same shall be taken care of by the concerned Anti-Encroachment Force strictly under the law. 3.

The respondent present in court has submitted that he has only made slops on the gate, as such there is no major encroachment over the street. Let the encroachment, if any, as pointed out be removed forthwith; however, if there is a need for certain steps to enter into the house, the same shall be taken care of by the concerned Anti-Encroachment Force strictly under the law. 3. This petition is disposed of in the above terms. JUDGE JUDGE *A.S*
